source.description: A reviewed six-panel graphite-and-colored-pencil process contact sheet.
source.notes: One consistent side-view swan gliding left on calm water, with a long S-curved neck, folded wing, small wake, and soft reflection; no second bird, cygnets, reeds, flowers, lettering, logo, decorative border, or full landscape.

process_strategy: The sequence locks one body oval, S-curved neck gesture, head-and-beak guides, folded-wing footprint, tail point, waterline, and reflection zone; wraps them with the swan silhouette; adds the face, beak division, layered wing feathers, ripples, wake, and reflection; then shades and colors only those established forms before final cleanup.

final_elements.name: body oval, S-curved neck gesture, head-and-beak guides, folded-wing footprint, tail point, waterline, and reflection zone
final_elements.description: Light construction fixes one left-facing swan, its graceful neck rhythm, wing placement, waterline, and the shallow reflected shape below.

final_elements.name: side-view swan silhouette with long neck, rounded body, head, tapered beak, folded wing, and pointed tail
final_elements.description: A relaxed contour turns the guides into one readable gliding swan while preserving the wing footprint and neck-to-body overlap.

final_elements.name: small eye, beak division, cheek line, three layered wing-feather groups, and underside feather edge
final_elements.description: A few specific landmarks give the swan expression and feather structure without rendering every feather.

final_elements.name: three water ripples, narrow trailing wake, soft broken reflection, and shallow contact shadow
final_elements.description: Calm horizontal marks seat the established bird on the water and echo its body without becoming a landscape.

final_elements.name: graphite depth with restrained blue-gray water and pale peach beak color
final_elements.description: Soft graphite, broken blue-gray pencil, and a small warm beak accent clarify only the existing swan, ripples, wake, and reflection.

frames.visible_job: Places the body oval, S-curved neck gesture, head circle, beak wedge, folded-wing footprint, tail point, waterline, and reflection zone at one fixed scale.
introduces: body oval, S-curved neck gesture, head-and-beak guides, folded-wing footprint, tail point, waterline, and reflection zone

frames.visible_job: Wraps the guides with the long neck, rounded body, head, tapered beak, folded wing, and pointed tail while keeping the same side-view pose.
introduces: side-view swan silhouette with long neck, rounded body, head, tapered beak, folded wing, and pointed tail
requires_prior_elements: body oval, S-curved neck gesture, head-and-beak guides, folded-wing footprint, tail point, waterline, and reflection zone

frames.visible_job: Adds the small eye, beak division, cheek line, three broad feather groups, and underside feather edge inside the completed silhouette.
introduces: small eye, beak division, cheek line, three layered wing-feather groups, and underside feather edge
requires_prior_elements: side-view swan silhouette with long neck, rounded body, head, tapered beak, folded wing, and pointed tail

frames.visible_job: Adds three horizontal ripples, a narrow trailing wake, a soft broken reflection, and a shallow contact shadow beneath the established bird.
introduces: three water ripples, narrow trailing wake, soft broken reflection, and shallow contact shadow
requires_prior_elements: side-view swan silhouette with long neck, rounded body, head, tapered beak, folded wing, and pointed tail

frames.visible_job: Adds soft graphite depth, broken blue-gray colored pencil, and a pale peach beak accent to the established swan, ripples, wake, and reflection.
introduces: graphite depth with restrained blue-gray water and pale peach beak color
requires_prior_elements: side-view swan silhouette with long neck, rounded body, head, tapered beak, folded wing, and pointed tail
small eye, beak division, cheek line, three layered wing-feather groups, and underside feather edge
three water ripples, narrow trailing wake, soft broken reflection, and shallow contact shadow

final_step.visible_job: Only strengthens the established swan contours, face, wing feathers, ripples, wake, reflection, restrained color, and paper highlights.
allowed_changes: restrained graphite and blue-gray colored-pencil shading
small highlight clarification on established feathers and water

rejection_checks: No head, beak, neck, body, wing, tail, eye, feather group, ripple, wake, reflection, shadow, or color area may first appear in the final panel.
The swan must retain one left-facing side view, S-curve, body proportion, wing placement, waterline, scale, and crop across all six panels.
Reserve the folded-wing footprint from step one; do not render finished body texture beneath the covering wing.
Reject extra birds, cygnets, reeds, flowers, lettering, logos, decorative borders, or full scenic backgrounds.
The finish must read as an attainable graphite-and-colored-pencil sketch with visible paper and hand pressure, not glossy wildlife art or a vector bird icon.
